html:
The Walking Cane is a Dress Tab Item, which can also be used as a Weapon. It requires 1 Walrus Tusk, 2 Gold Nuggets, and 4 Twigs to craft, and an Alchemy Engine to prototype. It increases the character's movement speed by 25% while equipped. The Walking Cane can be used to craft the Lazy Explorer.


Downloadable Content

In the Shipwrecked DLC, the Walking Cane cannot be crafted since MacTusk is not present. However, it can be obtained by using the Slot Machine, or as possible loot from the Quacken.

In the Hamlet DLC, the Walking Cane crafting recipe is unavailable, however, a Blueprint for it can be bought with 500 Oincs from The Tinkerer's Tower. The Walrus Tusk is available at 'The Sty' Oddities Emporium.

In both these DLCs, the Walking Cane can also be used while sailing on Boats, providing the same speed boost.

Usage

×2

×2

Tips

  • The Walking Cane can also be used as a weapon, although it is amongst the weakest in the game, as it only deals half the damage of a Spear when used as a weapon. However, it has an infinite durability, so it can be swung indefinitely.
  • The Walking Cane's speed boost puts the Character on the same level as many other creatures who could otherwise outrun them, including Followers like Chester. This is amplified by both natural and Cobblestone roads, for a combined increase of movement speed by 55%, allowing players to outrun most mobs in the game.
  • The Walking Cane is essential in some fights, as it allows the player to often pull out a single additional hit before needing to dodge the attack. For example, the bonus movement speed allows the player to deal more damage to Treeguards and all of seasonal giants, assuming the player is used to changing the weapon and the Walking Cane during the fight.
    • Thanks to its utility during fights, PC players are recommended to switch their weapon to the Walking Cane using the numbers on their keyboards when kiting (more preferably 1, 2 or 3 since they are close to the WASD keys), and then switch back to the weapon when hitting.
  • A Walking Cane's infinite durability and high attack speed makes it a decent weapon against some low HP and easy to stunlock mobs, such as Rabbits, Spiders, Bees, larger mobs that do not fight back such as the Lureplants or some very fragile entities such Ancient Fuelweaver's Woven Shadows.
  • One can stack the speed bonus with the Magiluminescence to have a total increase of 45% movement speed while both items are worn, which means that while on a road, the player will have a 75% speed boost. This can further be increased with WX-78's overload.
    • In the Shipwrecked DLC, speed can further be increased by eating Coffee and/or Tropical Bouillabaisse and by playing as Wilbur while on land.
    • In the Hamlet DLC, speed can further be increased by eating Iced Tea and/or Tea and by playing as Wheeler or Wormwood during blooming state.
  • The Walking Cane can be found on a Set Piece.

Trivia

  • The Walking Cane was added in The End Is Nigh update.
  • Walani's examine text for the Walking Cane is a reference to the Latin phrase "Veni, vidi, vici", which translates to "I came, I saw, I conquered".

Gallery

  • Walking Cane Equipped
  • Wilson wielding the Walking Cane.
  • A Walking Cane found near a skeleton in its natural set piece.
  • Walking Cane as seen in the poster for the The End is Nigh update.
Tools and WeaponsCraftable Items

“It makes walking seem much easier!”
–Wilson
“I can walk more easily with this!”
–Willow
“I can walk AND hit things!”
–Wolfgang
“I can get nowhere faster.”
–Wendy
“ASSISTED LOCOMOTION DEVICE”
–WX-78
“I'm no rabologist.”
–Wickerbottom
“It's good for the back country.”
–Woodie
“It has pictures of fast things carved into it.”
–Maxwell
“Amazing! It passes kinetic energy onto its user.”
–Wagstaff
“Turns "walk" tö "tröt".”
–Wigfrid
“One more point of contact couldn't slow us down.”
–Webber
“I cane, I saw.”
–Walani
“Now we are cooking with gas!”
–Warly
“I got me a built-in cane.”
–Woodlegs

–Wilba
“Friend for walking”
–Wormwood
“An efficient way to adventure.”
–Wheeler
“It's no Tin Lizzie.”
–Winona
“Is it walking me, or am I walking it?”
–Wortox
“This for old folk, florp.”
–Wurt
“Nothing like a good walking stick! Just don't chew on it this time, Woby.”
–Walter
“I don't need a cane yet! I feel fit as a fiddle!”
–Wanda
The Walking Cane is a Dress Tab Item, which can also be used as a Weapon. It requires 1 Walrus Tusk, 2 Gold Nuggets, and 4 Twigs to craft, and an Alchemy Engine to prototype. It increases the character's movement speed by 25% while equipped. The Walking Cane can be used to craft the Lazy Explorer.
Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
In the Shipwrecked DLC, the Walking Cane cannot be crafted since MacTusk is not present. However, it can be obtained by using the Slot Machine, or as possible loot from the Quacken.
In the Hamlet DLC, the Walking Cane crafting recipe is unavailable, however, a Blueprint for it can be bought with 500 Oincs from The Tinkerer's Tower. The Walrus Tusk is available at 'The Sty' Oddities Emporium.
In both these DLCs, the Walking Cane can also be used while sailing on Boats, providing the same speed boost.
Usage[edit | edit source]
×2
×2
Tips[edit | edit source]
The Walking Cane can also be used as a weapon, although it is amongst the weakest in the game, as it only deals half the damage of a Spear when used as a weapon. However, it has an infinite durability, so it can be swung indefinitely.
The Walking Cane's speed boost puts the Character on the same level as many other creatures who could otherwise outrun them, including Followers like Chester. This is amplified by both natural and Cobblestone roads, for a combined increase of movement speed by 55%, allowing players to outrun most mobs in the game.
The Walking Cane is essential in some fights, as it allows the player to often pull out a single additional hit before needing to dodge the attack. For example, the bonus movement speed allows the player to deal more damage to Treeguards and all of seasonal giants, assuming the player is used to changing the weapon and the Walking Cane during the fight.
Thanks to its utility during fights, PC players are recommended to switch their weapon to the Walking Cane using the numbers on their keyboards when kiting (more preferably 1, 2 or 3 since they are close to the WASD keys), and then switch back to the weapon when hitting.
A Walking Cane's infinite durability and high attack speed makes it a decent weapon against some low HP and easy to stunlock mobs, such as Rabbits, Spiders, Bees, larger mobs that do not fight back such as the Lureplants or some very fragile entities such Ancient Fuelweaver's Woven Shadows.
One can stack the speed bonus with the Magiluminescence to have a total increase of 45% movement speed while both items are worn, which means that while on a road, the player will have a 75% speed boost. This can further be increased with WX-78's overload.
In the Shipwrecked DLC, speed can further be increased by eating Coffee and/or Tropical Bouillabaisse and by playing as Wilbur while on land.
In the Hamlet DLC, speed can further be increased by eating Iced Tea and/or Tea and by playing as Wheeler or Wormwood during blooming state.
The Walking Cane can be found on a Set Piece.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Walking Cane was added in The End Is Nigh update.
Walani's examine text for the Walking Cane is a reference to the Latin phrase "Veni, vidi, vici", which translates to "I came, I saw, I conquered".

The '''Walking Cane''' is a [[Dress Tab]] [[Item]], which can also be used as a [[Weapon]]. It requires 1 [[Walrus Tusk]], 2 [[Gold Nugget]]s, and 4 [[Twigs]] to craft, and an [[Alchemy Engine]] to prototype. It increases the character's movement [[Player Speed|speed]] by 25% while equipped. The Walking Cane can be used to craft the [[The Lazy Explorer|Lazy Explorer]].

__NOTOC__
== {{Pic32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content ==
In the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC, the Walking Cane cannot be crafted since [[MacTusk]] is not present. However, it can be obtained by using the [[Slot Machine]], or as possible loot from the [[Quacken]].

In the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC, the Walking Cane crafting recipe is unavailable, however, a [[Blueprint]] for it can be bought with 500 [[Oinc]]s from [[The Tinkerer's Tower]]. The Walrus Tusk is available at [['The Sty' Oddities Emporium]].

In both these DLCs, the Walking Cane can also be used while sailing on [[Boats]], providing the same speed boost.

=={{pic|32|Icon Tools}} Usage==
{{Recipe
|item2 = Nightmare Fuel
|count2 = 2
|item1 = Walking Cane
|item3 = Orange Gem
|count3 = 2
|result = The Lazy Explorer
|tool = Ancient Pseudoscience Station}}

=={{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips ==
* The Walking Cane can also be used as a weapon, although it is amongst the weakest in the game, as it only deals half the damage of a [[Spear]] when used as a weapon. However, it has an infinite durability, so it can be swung indefinitely.
*The Walking Cane's speed boost puts the [[Character]] on the same level as many other creatures who could otherwise outrun them, including [[Followers]] like [[Chester]]. This is amplified by both [[Trail|natural]] and [[Cobblestones|Cobblestone]] roads, for a combined increase of movement speed by 55%, allowing players to outrun most mobs in the game.
*The Walking Cane is essential in some fights, as it allows the player to often pull out a single additional hit before needing to dodge the attack. For example, the bonus movement speed allows the player to deal more damage to [[Treeguard|Treeguards]] and all of seasonal giants, assuming the player is used to changing the weapon and the Walking Cane during the fight.
**Thanks to its utility during fights, PC players are recommended to switch their weapon to the Walking Cane using the numbers on their keyboards when kiting (more preferably 1, 2 or 3 since they are close to the WASD keys), and then switch back to the weapon when hitting.
*A Walking Cane's infinite durability and high attack speed makes it a decent weapon against some low HP and easy to stunlock mobs, such as [[Rabbit|Rabbits]], [[Spiders]], [[Bee|Bees]], larger mobs that do not fight back such as the [[Meat Bulb (Lureplant)|Lureplants]] or some very fragile entities such [[Reanimated Skeleton|Ancient Fuelweaver's]] Woven Shadows.
* One can stack the speed bonus with the [[Magiluminescence]] to have a total increase of 45% movement speed while both items are worn, which means that while on a road, the player will have a 75% speed boost. This can further be increased with [[WX-78]]'s overload.
** In the ''Shipwrecked'' DLC, speed can further be increased by eating [[Coffee]] and/or [[Tropical Bouillabaisse]] and by playing as [[Wilbur]] while on land.
** In the ''Hamlet'' DLC, speed can further be increased by eating [[Iced Tea]] and/or [[Tea]] and by playing as [[Wheeler]] or [[Wormwood]] during blooming state.
* The Walking Cane can be found on a [[Set Piece]].

=={{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia==
* The Walking Cane was added in ''[[Version History#The End is Nigh|The End Is Nigh]]'' update.
*Walani's examine text for the Walking Cane is a reference to the Latin phrase "Veni, vidi, vici", which translates to "I came, I saw, I conquered".
